vllm.title: [HTTP Server] Make model param optional in request
vllm.author: youngkent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We often have single model per deployment setup. Requiring model name in request create unnecessary coupling between service and client. This change make "model" param optional. When not given, it uses the first available model on the server to serve the request.
Test before the change, one need to

vllm.title: [Bugfix][API Server] Fix invalid usage of 'ge' and 'le' in port valid…
vllm.author: WangErXiao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix invalid usage of 'ge' and 'le' in port validation in #13506 ,
which cause "TypeError: _StoreAction.init() got an unexpected keyword argument 'ge'".

vllm.title: [Misc] Capture and log the time of loading weights
vllm.author: waltforme
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR tries to introduce more transparency to weights loading.
Today we're already logging the memory footprint Loading model weights took X GB.
We can do even better by also logging the time consumption like Loading model weights took X seconds.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Model] OLMo 2: split qkv correctly for GQA and MQA
vllm.author: 2015aroras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Issue: OLMo 2 does not correctly do attention when the number of heads is not the same as the number of kv heads (i.e. GQA or MQA is used instead of MHA). Specifically, it splits qkv into equal chunks rather than chunks based on q, k, v size.
Fix: Split qkv based on q, k, v size.
Testing: We ran thi

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix benchmark script bug: inaccurate stats for vllm backend when max_model_len < input_len + output_len
vllm.author: WangErXiao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix benchmark script bug: inaccurate stats for vllm backend when max_model_len < input_len + output_len.
When max_model_len is smaller than prompt_len + expected_output_len, the request may terminate early without reaching the expected_output_len, resulting in inaccurate throughput statistics. This

vllm.title: [v1] Support allowed_token_ids in v1 Sampler
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow the implementation in vllm/entrypoints/openai/logits_processors.py.
The idea is straightforward, adding a [batch_size x vocab_size] mask tensor, and leverage a list of bools to determine whether to do the inplace masked fill.

 add test
 move some verification in _get_allowed_token_ids_logits
